---
title: "|
  STAT 408
  
  Lab 7: Logicals and data manipulation"
author: "Jess Kunke"
date: "Thu Feb 26 2026"
date-format: "ddd DD MMM YYYY"
format:
  revealjs:
    html-math-method: mathjax
    footer: "STAT 408 Computing, Spring 2026"
    transition: fade
    title-slide-attributes: 
      data-background-color: "teal"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 1
    toc-title: "Outline"
    number-sections: false
    css: "custom.css"
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    auto-stretch: false
    # fontsize: 20pt
    # title-slide-attributes: 
    #   data-background-image: background.jpg
---

## Today's lab: Overview {.smaller}

1. (10 min) Divide into 6 groups, and within your group, write 2 survey questions per person.

2. (10 min) Join one other group and combine your survey questions into one Google Form, resulting in 3 surveys for the class.

3. (10 min) Everyone takes all 3 surveys.

4. Within groups, download the data from your survey as a csv file, read it into R, and analyze it.


## Step 1 (10 minutes) {.smaller}

Divide into 6 groups. Within your group, work together to write 2 survey questions per person. Make sure to include at least one question of each of the following types:

- Open-ended short-answer text (example: What department/program are you in?)
- Open-ended long-form text (example: Who is the best basketball player of all time and why?)
- Multiple choice with text answers (example: What is your snowboarding experience level?)
- Integer (example: How old are you?)
- Numeric, possibly decimal (example: What is your height in feet? Please round to the nearest 0.1 foot.)

Don't make your questions too personal/identifying/sensitive, or inappropriate for class. While the data will be anonymous, we will be sharing it with the class to read and analyze.

Make sure you have enough variety to your questions that the data will be interesting/fun to analyze.

## Step 2 (10 minutes) {.smaller}

Join up with one other group (3 groups total). Combine your questions together and enter them into a Google Form. Two options for edit access:

- One person has edit access. Once the data is collected, they will download the data and share the file with everyone in the group.
- Everyone in the group has edit access. Once the data is collected, everyone can download the data themselves.

When you're done, publish the survey, click "Copy Responder Link", select the option for a short link, and post it on the Canvas discussion board.


## Step 3 (10 minutes) {.smaller}

Everyone take all three surveys!

- Leave one question blank on one or two of the surveys so we'll have some missing data
- Keep your answers appropriate

## Analyzing the data {.smaller}

As a class, we now have three survey datasets.

- Each group needs to download the Google Form data and post it on the Canvas discussion board so others can access it
- Read into R the dataset from the survey your group designed
- Follow the lab instructions for your analysis
